narrative:
Skies are mostly cloudy with scattered showers and thunderstorms this afternoon. By day's end, I think most spots wind up with some rain, but amounts will be highly variable and could be locally heavy. We can't rule out a hefty storm with gusty winds or a little hail, as well. Thursday will feature noticeably more sunshine, with just hit-or-miss action during the afternoon. Thereafter, Friday through the weekend will be predominantly dry, but we have to keep a stray mention going with weak disturbances in play. Temperatures will be rather close to normal for this time of year, but cooling off some by late in the weekend.

The Memorial Day Monday Message is now available to subscribers. Drought relief is our top topic as the water faucet is back on!

2027 Blue Ridge Photo Winners Announced

The votes are in, and the winning photos have been selected. The Grand Prize Winner is a fall photo by Jason Frye of Price Lake looking toward Grandfather Mountain. See this and all the other winners at our Photo Contest Page.

Scattered Showers & Thunderstorms

Last Updated Wed May 27 at 3:52PM by Alan Simmons

Skies are mostly cloudy with scattered showers and thunderstorms this afternoon. By day's end, I think most spots wind up with some rain, but amounts will be highly variable and could be locally heavy. We can't rule out a hefty storm with gusty winds or a little hail, as well. Thursday will feature noticeably more sunshine, with just hit-or-miss action during the afternoon. Thereafter, Friday through the weekend will be predominantly dry, but we have to keep a stray mention going with weak disturbances in play. Temperatures will be rather close to normal for this time of year, but cooling off some by late in the weekend.

The Memorial Day Monday Message is now available to subscribers. Drought relief is our top topic as the water faucet is back on!

2027 Blue Ridge Photo Winners Announced

The votes are in, and the winning photos have been selected. The Grand Prize Winner is a fall photo by Jason Frye of Price Lake looking toward Grandfather Mountain. See this and all the other winners at our Photo Contest Page.

Forecast Discussion

A moist and rather unstable air mass in place over western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia will give rise to more scattered showers and thunderstorms, mostly this afternoon. One or two spots could get a gully washer, along with gusty winds and perhaps even a bit of small hail.

A cold front settles southward through the region during Thursday, with more widely-separated showers and thundershowers. Our low-to-mid level wind flow will veer from southerly to more westerly. That’s a drier regime that will allow for more sunshine.

Friday looks like a predominantly dry day with ample sunshine and only a stray afternoon pop-up shower. A wave of low pressure along the stalled-out front to our south should keep most rainfall out of our region for the weekend. We will keep an isolated shower for Saturday, but Sunday looks dry.

A stronger upper level disturbance and associated surface wave of low-pressure may provide a better chance for showers for the early part of next week.

The air mass early next week will be uncharacteristically cool for the beginning of June.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for May 25, 2026

It's the Memorial Day Monday Message, where we recap our sorely-needed drought relief and discuss our region's cyclical warm/cool periods.

May 25, 2026
Rainy, Cool Saturday

It's a rainy, cool Saturday around western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia. High temperatures are running as much as 20-degrees below normal in the heart of the cold air damming zone.

Sunday and Memorial Day will continue to be unsettled, but temperatures will moderate and there will be additional breaks in the clouds, as the rainfall pattern becomes more convective, or showery in nature.

While the rainfall is falling on our holiday weekend, it's entirely beneficial with respect to our long-standing drought conditions.
